titlestmt.titleproper: New York State College of Human Ecology Ghana Project records23-2-2703
titlestmt.author: Compiled by KJ Hillgren

publicationstmt.publisher: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

ead.archdesc:
======= RMA02703New York State College of Human Ecology Ghana Project records23-2-2703Compiled by KJ HillgrenDivision of Rare and Manuscript Collections
January 20042B Carl A. Kroch LibraryCornell UniversityIthaca, NY 14853rareref@cornell.eduURL:
humec afrcaThis finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2022-09-19 18:26:03 UTC.12/07/2018
This resource was modified by the ArchivesSpace Preprocessor developed by the Harvard Library (https://github.com/harvard-library/archivesspace-preprocessor)2007-05-29
converted from EAD 1.0 to EAD 2002 >>>>>>> 2e3ec5c57314077b8435567632a49d313357daab
English
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections
New York State College of Human Ecology Ghana Project records
New York State College of Human Ecology. Ghana Project
Rhodes, Kathleen.
23-2-2703
1.1 cubic feet. 1.1 cubic feet.
1961-1988. Reports, correspondence, lists, syllabi, plans, clippings, photographs, and other materials. Ghana Project records, including description of the project, reports, correspondence, lists, syllabi, plans, clippings, and photographs and the work done by Professor Kathleen Rhodes. Collection material in English 2248607
General
Contact Information:
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524 rareref@cornell.edu http://rmc.library.cornell.edu
Compiled by:
KJ Hillgren
Date completed:
November 2003
EAD encoding:
KJ Hillgren, Martin Heggestad, January 2004
COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
Ghana Project records, including description of the project, reports, correspondence, lists, syllabi, plans, clippings, and photographs and the work done by Professor Kathleen Rhodes.
SERIES LIST
Series I. Photographs A. Reception Photos B. Picnic and Barbecue Photos C. Ithaca Sr. High Photos D. Extension Classes and Workshop Photos E. Miscellaneous Photos
Series II. Slides
Series III. Documents
Series IV. Memorabilia
Cite As:
New York State College of Human Ecology Ghana Project records, #23-2-2703. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.
Ghana -- Study and teaching. Africa -- American influences. African cooperation. Home economics -- International cooperation. International cooperation.

SlidesKenya Slides27-9Nigeria Slides210-12Miscellaneous Slides213-14Series III. DocumentsDescription of Project; "The International Heritage of Home Economics in USA" by Kathleen Rhodes, AHEA-Ghana.198811Progress reports on the Cornell-Ghana project1964-196611
Scope and Contents
Includes progress report 1 from 1964, report 2 from 1965 and report 3 from 1966.Closing words spoken at the reception of the National Education Association 1961-12-0612Report by Kathleen Rhodes on visit to Ghana1962-11-07-1962-11-2712Proposed projects in Ghana1963-0112Correspondence1962-196312Survey Conducted by Students at Winneba College and student autobiographies; April 1963 Report on 2nd Visit to Ghana by Kathleen Rhodes196313Correspondence regarding Agreement with Ghana Steering committee minutes.May 196314Project Statement (revised); Oct., Nov., Dec. correspondence.196315Correspondence and reports from Ghana196416Plans for and reports of consultant conference in Ghana; Reports from consultants, needs of Home Science program and proposed budget; Arrangements for visitors from Ghana. Report from Wood on Ghana situation. Correspondence regarding Home Demonstration Agents participation.196417Proposed phases of Cornell-Ghana project; Correspondence regarding Rhodes visits to Ghana; Report by Flemming (University of Ghana); Arrangements for visit by Jiagge (Ghana); Report regarding Cornell-Ghana project Wilson.196418Advisory Committee minutes; Regulations for Diploma in Home Science; Needs of Home Science Program (Rhodes); statement of project; Reports (Newkirk and Feldman)196419Letters from Newkirk; clippings from Ghana Times; Advisory Committee minutes, personnel recruitment for '65-'66.1964110Summary report Sept.-Jan. (Newkirk); Letters to and from Newkirk regarding salary funding.1965111Letters from Narrowsburg Home Demonstration unit; Colson (U of Ghana) to Wilson (U of Ghana); Flemming (U of Ghana) to Colson.1965112Advisory Committee Minutes (3/3/65); Letters Canoyer (CU) to Brown (Ghana); Wood to Newkirk; Wilson to Canoyer; Wood to Flemming; Canoyer to Doulo (Ghana); Newkirk to Wood; Wood to Armbruster; O'Brien (U of Ghana) to Wood, Newkirk to Damatey (Ghana); Feldman and Newkirk to Wood; Canoyer to O'Brien.1965113Institutions Cooperating with CU-Ghana program. Advisory Committee minutes; letters Canoyer to Miller (Ghana); Canoyer to Pres. Perkins; Newkirk to Wood; Project report Feb.-March (Newkirk, memoranda on future projects, review of commitments)1965114Advisory Comm. memo; report to college faculty and minutes; correspondence need for Home Science degree; recommendations regarding Home Science (U of Ghana).1965115Letters Wood and Newkirk; Communications regarding project development.1965116Reports of social science consultant; memo of understanding (CU and U of Ghana); Letters Muller (CU) to Canoyer; Wood to Edzii (U of Ghana) and Newkirk; Newkirk to Wood; Advisory Committee minutes.1965117Letters, Canoyer to Middlemast (H Dagent); Muller to Wood; Wood to Olds; Ambassador Williams; Newkirk; Kwapong (U of Ghana); Anderegg; Review of memo of understanding and budget.1965118Memos to Advisory Committee regarding plans for HSC building at U of Ghana; award of Fulbright to V. Cukler for 66-67; letter Canoyer to Addo (Ghana)1966119Report (Newkirk); letter Newkirk regarding building plans; Advisory Committee minutes; Revised draft of Memo of Understanding.1966120Comments on Ghana contract and memo of understanding; Final report on Cornell-Ghana project.1966121Letter Wood to Cutter; memos from Cutter; draft of proposed site visit to U of Ghana by USAID.1966122Advisory Committee memo regarding planning HSC facilities at University of Ghana; description of program for FAO; letters between Wood and Cutter; Statements; "Education of Women in Ghana"; "Women's Education and Development of Community Health."1967-1968123Winneba College syllabi; Nutrition; Applied Chemistry and Physics; Regulations for 1963; Plan for '65 grads.1963124Winneba College Regulations; statement of project.1964125Winneba College syllabi for Foods and Nutrition; Social Science; Regulations for Home Science Diploma1965-1966126Materials on clothing and textiles for home science diploma.127-28Research Studies; Home Science course 1964-19651964-1965129Requests for financial assistance1962-1965130Newspaper clippings: Ghana1964131-32Newspaper clippings: Ghana1965133-34Newspaper clippings: Ghana1966135-36Lati Hyde-Forster's closing speech in a letter from Helen G.
